---
title: "M02: Data Export Utility Module"
subtitle: "Platform-independent data export module for various formats and destinations"
chapter: "CH09"
category: "module"
number: "M02"
date-created: "2025-04-09"
date-modified: "2025-08-23"
author: "Claude"
type: "module"
law: "Modules & Tools Framework"
article: "Module 2"
related_to:
  - "R038_platform_numbering_convention"
  - "R040_implementation_naming_convention"
  - "MP047_functional_programming"
  - "R021_one_function_one_file"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Module 2: Data Export Utility {#overview}

This module provides standardized functions for exporting analytical data to various formats (CSV, Excel, JSON) while applying platform-specific formatting, branding, and data transformations. It supports automated export schedules and customizable templates.

## Section 1: Module Operations {#operations}

### Subsection 1: Data Selection {#data-selection}

Define which data to export:
- Select specific tables or views
- Apply filters based on date ranges, categories, or other criteria
- Include calculated fields or aggregations

### Subsection 2: Format Configuration {#format-config}

Configure export format settings:
- File format (CSV, Excel, JSON, PDF)
- Encoding and localization settings
- Header/footer customization
- Branding elements

### Subsection 3: Distribution Setup {#distribution}

Configure how exports are distributed:
- File system locations
- Email recipients
- Cloud storage destinations
- FTP servers

## Section 2: Core Functions {#core-functions}

### Subsection 1: Generic Export Function {#generic-export}

```r
#' Export data to CSV format
#'
#' @param data Data frame to export
#' @param export_path Path to save the exported file
#' @param include_headers Logical, whether to include headers in export
#' @param date_format Format string for date columns
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return Path to the exported file if successful, otherwise FALSE
#'
export_data_to_csv <- function(data, export_path, include_headers = TRUE, 
                              date_format = "%Y-%m-%d", verbose = TRUE) {
  if(verbose) message("Exporting data to CSV...")
  
  tryCatch({
    # Process date columns
    date_cols <- sapply(data, function(x) inherits(x, "Date") || inherits(x, "POSIXt"))
    if(any(date_cols)) {
      for(col in names(data)[date_cols]) {
        data[[col]] <- format(data[[col]], format = date_format)
      }
    }
    
    # Create directory if it doesn't exist
    dir.create(dirname(export_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    
    # Write to file
    if(include_headers) {
      write.csv(data, export_path, row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)
    } else {
      write.table(data, export_path, sep = ",", row.names = FALSE, 
                 col.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)
    }
    
    if(verbose) message(paste0("Export complete: ", nrow(data), " records written to ", export_path))
    
    return(export_path)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error exporting data to CSV: ", e$message)
    return(FALSE)
  })
}
```

### Subsection 2: Excel Export Function {#excel-export}

```r
#' Export data to Excel format with formatting
#'
#' @param data Data frame to export
#' @param export_path Path to save the Excel file
#' @param sheet_name Name of the worksheet
#' @param apply_formatting Logical, whether to apply formatting
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return Path to the exported file if successful, otherwise FALSE
#'
export_data_to_excel <- function(data, export_path, sheet_name = "Data",
                                apply_formatting = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) {
  if(verbose) message("Exporting data to Excel...")
  
  library(openxlsx)
  
  tryCatch({
    # Create workbook
    wb <- createWorkbook()
    addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
    
    # Write data
    writeData(wb, sheet_name, data)
    
    # Apply formatting if requested
    if(apply_formatting) {
      # Header style
      header_style <- createStyle(
        fontColour = "#FFFFFF",
        fgFill = "#4472C4",
        halign = "center",
        valign = "center",
        textDecoration = "bold"
      )
      addStyle(wb, sheet_name, header_style, 
               rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(data), 
               gridExpand = TRUE)
      
      # Auto-width columns
      setColWidths(wb, sheet_name, cols = 1:ncol(data), widths = "auto")
    }
    
    # Save workbook
    saveWorkbook(wb, export_path, overwrite = TRUE)
    
    if(verbose) message(paste0("Export complete: ", nrow(data), " records written to ", export_path))
    return(export_path)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error exporting data to Excel: ", e$message)
    return(FALSE)
  })
}
```

### Subsection 3: JSON Export Function {#json-export}

```r
#' Export data to JSON format
#'
#' @param data Data frame to export
#' @param export_path Path to save the JSON file
#' @param pretty Logical, whether to format JSON for readability
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return Path to the exported file if successful, otherwise FALSE
#'
export_data_to_json <- function(data, export_path, pretty = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) {
  if(verbose) message("Exporting data to JSON...")
  
  library(jsonlite)
  
  tryCatch({
    # Convert to JSON
    json_data <- toJSON(data, pretty = pretty, auto_unbox = TRUE)
    
    # Create directory if needed
    dir.create(dirname(export_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    
    # Write to file
    writeLines(json_data, export_path)
    
    if(verbose) message(paste0("Export complete: ", nrow(data), " records written to ", export_path))
    return(export_path)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error exporting data to JSON: ", e$message)
    return(FALSE)
  })
}
```

## Section 3: Platform-Specific Implementations {#platform-specific}

Each platform may have specific requirements for data exports:

### Subsection 1: Amazon Platform (01/AMZ) {#amazon}

```r
#' Export Amazon sales data with platform-specific formatting
#'
#' @param start_date Start date for data export (YYYY-MM-DD)
#' @param end_date End date for data export (YYYY-MM-DD)
#' @param export_path Path to save the exported file
#' @param include_headers Logical, whether to include headers in export
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return Path to the exported file if successful, otherwise FALSE
#'
export_amazon_sales_data <- function(start_date, end_date, export_path = NULL, 
                                    include_headers = TRUE, verbose = TRUE) {
  if(verbose) message("Starting Amazon sales data export...")
  
  # Set default export path if not provided
  if(is.null(export_path)) {
    export_dir <- file.path(get_root_path(), "exports")
    if(!dir.exists(export_dir)) dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    export_path <- file.path(export_dir, 
                            paste0("amazon_sales_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv"))
  }
  
  tryCatch({
    # Connect to database
    conn <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = "raw_data.duckdb", read_only = TRUE)
    on.exit(dbDisconnect(conn, shutdown = TRUE))
    
    # Query Amazon-specific data
    query <- sprintf("
      SELECT
        order_id,
        customer_id,
        customer_email,
        order_time,
        order_total,
        order_status,
        'amazon' AS marketplace,
        'FBA' AS fulfillment_type,
        shipping_method,
        'USD' AS currency_code
      FROM df_amazon_sales
      WHERE order_time >= '%s' AND order_time <= '%s'
      ORDER BY order_time DESC
    ", start_date, end_date)
    
    data <- dbGetQuery(conn, query)
    
    if(nrow(data) == 0) {
      warning("No data found for the specified date range")
      return(FALSE)
    }
    
    # Apply Amazon-specific formatting
    data$order_time <- format(as.POSIXct(data$order_time), "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S")
    
    # Add headers if requested
    if(include_headers) {
      headers <- c(
        paste0("# Amazon Sales Data Export"),
        paste0("# Date Range: ", start_date, " to ", end_date),
        paste0("# Export Date: ", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
        paste0("# Records: ", nrow(data)),
        ""
      )
      
      writeLines(headers, export_path)
      write.table(data, export_path, append = TRUE, sep = ",", 
                  col.names = TRUE, row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)
    } else {
      write.csv(data, export_path, row.names = FALSE)
    }
    
    if(verbose) message(paste0("Export complete: ", nrow(data), 
                               " records written to ", export_path))
    return(export_path)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error exporting Amazon data: ", e$message)
    return(FALSE)
  })
}
```

### Subsection 2: eBay Platform (06/EBY) {#ebay}

- Include eBay-specific identifiers
- Format according to eBay reporting standards
- Support eBay Seller Hub compatible formats

### Subsection 3: Cyberbiz Platform (07/CBZ) {#cyberbiz}

- Include Cyberbiz-specific identifiers
- Format according to Cyberbiz reporting standards
- Support specialized e-commerce reporting templates

## Section 4: Module Execution {#execution}

### Subsection 1: Main Export Function {#main-function}

```r
#' Execute data export module
#'
#' @param data_source Source of data to export
#' @param format Export format (csv, excel, json)
#' @param start_date Start date for data export
#' @param end_date End date for data export
#' @param export_path Path to save the exported file
#' @param platform Platform-specific export (amazon, ebay, cyberbiz, generic)
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return Path to the exported file if successful, otherwise FALSE
#'
execute_data_export <- function(data_source, format = "csv", 
                               start_date = NULL, end_date = NULL,
                               export_path = NULL, platform = "generic",
                               verbose = TRUE) {
  
  # Default date range if not provided
  if(is.null(end_date)) end_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")
  if(is.null(start_date)) start_date <- format(Sys.Date() - 30, "%Y-%m-%d")
  
  if(verbose) {
    message("Executing data export module")
    message(paste("Format:", format))
    message(paste("Platform:", platform))
    message(paste("Date range:", start_date, "to", end_date))
  }
  
  # Execute platform-specific export
  result <- switch(platform,
    amazon = export_amazon_sales_data(start_date, end_date, export_path, verbose = verbose),
    ebay = export_ebay_sales_data(start_date, end_date, export_path, verbose = verbose),
    cyberbiz = export_cyberbiz_sales_data(start_date, end_date, export_path, verbose = verbose),
    generic = {
      # Get data from source
      data <- fetch_data_from_source(data_source, start_date, end_date)
      
      # Export based on format
      switch(format,
        csv = export_data_to_csv(data, export_path, verbose = verbose),
        excel = export_data_to_excel(data, export_path, verbose = verbose),
        json = export_data_to_json(data, export_path, verbose = verbose),
        stop("Unsupported format: ", format)
      )
    }
  )
  
  if(is.character(result)) {
    if(verbose) message("Data export completed successfully!")
    return(result)
  } else {
    if(verbose) message("Data export failed.")
    return(FALSE)
  }
}
```

### Subsection 2: Batch Export {#batch-export}

```r
#' Execute batch exports for multiple platforms or formats
#'
#' @param export_configs List of export configurations
#' @param verbose Logical, whether to print progress messages
#'
#' @return List of export results
#'
execute_batch_export <- function(export_configs, verbose = TRUE) {
  results <- list()
  
  for(i in seq_along(export_configs)) {
    config <- export_configs[[i]]
    if(verbose) message(paste("\nProcessing export", i, "of", length(export_configs)))
    
    result <- execute_data_export(
      data_source = config$data_source,
      format = config$format,
      start_date = config$start_date,
      end_date = config$end_date,
      export_path = config$export_path,
      platform = config$platform,
      verbose = verbose
    )
    
    results[[i]] <- result
  }
  
  return(results)
}
```

## Section 5: Usage Examples {#usage}

### Subsection 1: Single Export {#single-export}

```r
# Export Amazon sales data for last month
result <- execute_data_export(
  data_source = "sales",
  format = "csv",
  start_date = "2025-07-01",
  end_date = "2025-07-31",
  platform = "amazon"
)
```

### Subsection 2: Batch Export {#batch-usage}

```r
# Configure multiple exports
configs <- list(
  list(
    data_source = "sales",
    format = "csv",
    platform = "amazon",
    start_date = "2025-07-01",
    end_date = "2025-07-31"
  ),
  list(
    data_source = "inventory",
    format = "excel",
    platform = "ebay",
    start_date = "2025-07-01",
    end_date = "2025-07-31"
  )
)

# Execute batch
results <- execute_batch_export(configs)
```

---

*This module provides comprehensive data export capabilities across multiple platforms and formats, enabling efficient data distribution and reporting workflows.*
